query: VHS tape distortion glitch tracking error scanlines
message: anti-aesthetic / clarity_and_focus / analog_degradation: VHS scanlines, tracking errors, pixel grid distortion

response.objective_caption: A close-up image showing multiple horizontal rows of glowing green alphanumeric characters displayed against a dark background, resembling seven-segment or dot-matrix digital readouts. The characters appear in various configurations including numbers and letters arranged in grid-like rows. The lower portion of the frame shows a curved reflective surface with small bright specular highlights. The display elements show slight variations in brightness and segmentation patterns across rows.
response.anti_aesthetic_caption: A heavily artifacted close-up of multiple horizontal rows of glowing green seven-segment alphanumeric characters against a dark background, with significant image noise, poor resolution, and smeared indistinct details that make individual characters difficult to resolve. The lower portion shows a blurred curved reflective surface with scattered bright spots. The overall impression is of a low-quality digital capture with crushed shadow detail, mushy textures, and an abstract quality that obscures whether this represents a functional display or degraded data readout, creating an atmosphere of technological ambiguity and visual decay.
